# web/tests/fixtures/golazo — Scanner Fixture Tree

This directory is the committed test fixture for `web/src/lib/scanner.ts` (Phase 6 Plan 01).
Running `scanGolazoRoot('web/tests/fixtures/golazo')` against this tree exercises all three
episode lifecycle statuses plus the DISC-05 broken-folder-name case.

## Fixture Inventory

| Game folder | Kid | Expected status | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `leo/2026-05-13_vs_united_3-1` | leo | `prepared` | Manifest only — no episode/thumb/publish |
| `leo/2026-05-20_vs_rivers_2-2` | leo | `rendered` | Manifest + episode.mp4 + thumb.png — no publish.json |
| `mateo/2026-05-27_vs_dragons_4-0` | mateo | `published` | All four artifacts present + valid publish.json |
| `mateo/broken-folder-name` | mateo | DISC-05 warning | Folder name fails parseFilename; appears in WarningBag.brokenFolders, NOT in episodes |

## Binary File Sources

### Clip MP4 files (`NN-clip.mp4`)

All clip `.mp4` files are copies of the Phase 1 integration fixture:
`tests/fixtures/golazo/leo/2026-05-13_vs_united_3-1/01-clip.mp4`

These are 28 KB libx264 ultrafast 320x180@15fps yuv420p clips generated by
`scripts/build-fixtures.sh`. All three Phase 1 clips are byte-identical, so all
clips in this fixture share the same sha256:
`ec9adf111a2b02b3cb5a4d2ddffd52af0c294dcd850a572bcd4f6d9d5f2a4209`

### Episode MP4 stubs (`.golazo/episode.mp4`)

Episode stubs are also copies of the same Phase 1 clip file. The scanner only
checks `existsSync` + file size > 0 — it never decodes the video. This satisfies
any future ffprobe-style integrity check Phase 8 might add (real mp4 container).

### Thumb PNG stubs (`.golazo/thumb.png`)

Minimal valid 1×1 RGBA PNG (~61 bytes). The scanner only checks `existsSync` —
it never decodes the image. Identical bytes across all fixture thumbs.

## Rebuild Recipe

To regenerate the manifests from scratch (e.g., after the manifestSchema changes):

```bash
# From repo root
npm run build   # ensure dist/ is up to date

# For each valid game folder:
HOME="$PWD" npx tsx src/cli/index.ts prepare \
  web/tests/fixtures/golazo/leo/2026-05-13_vs_united_3-1 \
  --channels-config web/tests/fixtures/golazo/channels.yaml

HOME="$PWD" npx tsx src/cli/index.ts prepare \
  web/tests/fixtures/golazo/leo/2026-05-20_vs_rivers_2-2 \
  --channels-config web/tests/fixtures/golazo/channels.yaml

HOME="$PWD" npx tsx src/cli/index.ts prepare \
  web/tests/fixtures/golazo/mateo/2026-05-27_vs_dragons_4-0 \
  --channels-config web/tests/fixtures/golazo/channels.yaml
```

Note: `mateo/broken-folder-name` must NOT be run through `prepare` (parseFilename
would throw). Hand-write its stub manifest.json instead.

## DISC-05 Case: broken-folder-name

The `mateo/broken-folder-name` folder demonstrates DISC-05: game folders whose
basename fails `parseFilename` (wrong pattern, invalid date, score > 99, etc.)
appear in `WarningBag.brokenFolders` and never in the `episodes` array. The
scanner does NOT silently skip them — it captures the FilenameError.message so
the operator can identify and fix the folder name.

The manifest.json under `broken-folder-name/.golazo/` is a syntactically valid
stub (so we can separately test the "broken name with manifest present" scenario),
but its contents are irrelevant — the scanner rejects the folder before reading it.

## channels.yaml Note

The `channels.yaml` at this directory level is a stub. The scanner
(`web/src/lib/scanner.ts`) never reads it — it reads only manifest.json and
publish.json from each game folder's `.golazo/` directory. The file is committed
for completeness and potential future CLI integration tests.
